name: Geography
description: A survey of physical and human geography — Earth's landforms, climate zones, ecosystems, world regions, cultural patterns, economic systems, and the relationship between people and place.

modules.title: Introduction to Geography
modules.objectives: Identify the five themes of geography; explain how maps, GPS, and geographic tools help us understand the world; locate places using latitude and longitude.
modules.readingNote: National Geographic Education — What is Geography?
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Geography #1 — What Is Geography?
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Maps and Geographic Inquiry
modules.assignment: Choose any location on Earth. Use latitude and longitude to identify it, then describe it using all five themes of geography (location, place, human-environment interaction, movement, region).

modules.title: Physical Geography: Landforms
modules.objectives: Identify the seven continents and major landforms; explain how tectonic plates shape Earth's surface; describe the formation of mountains, rivers, and plains.
modules.assignment: Create a labeled diagram of Earth's major landform types (mountains, plains, plateaus, valleys, rivers). For each, name one real-world example and the continent where it is found.

modules.title: Climate & Weather Patterns
modules.objectives: Distinguish between weather and climate; identify the major climate zones using the Köppen classification system; explain factors that influence climate.
modules.practiceNote: National Geographic Education — Weather vs. Climate
modules.assignment: Compare two cities with different climate types (e.g., Singapore vs. Cairo). For each city, identify the Köppen classification, describe typical seasonal conditions, and explain what geographic factors cause those conditions.

modules.title: World Ecosystems
modules.objectives: Identify major world biomes including rainforests, deserts, grasslands, and tundra; explain how climate determines ecosystem type; describe key plant and animal adaptations.
modules.assignment: Select two contrasting biomes (e.g., tropical rainforest and tundra). Create a comparison table covering climate, plant life, animal life, and human impact. Then write a paragraph explaining how each biome's climate shapes its living community.

modules.title: Human Geography: Population
modules.objectives: Explain global population distribution and density patterns; analyze demographic trends including birth rate, death rate, and natural increase; understand urbanization.
modules.practiceNote: CIA World Factbook — Country Comparisons (Population Data)
modules.assignment: Using CIA World Factbook data, compare the population density, birth rate, and urbanization rate of one high-income country and one low-income country. Write three sentences explaining what these numbers reveal about each country's stage of development.

modules.title: Culture & Identity
modules.objectives: Define culture and its components; explain how language, religion, and ethnicity shape cultural regions; analyze cultural diffusion and the effects of globalization.
modules.assignment: Identify one example of cultural diffusion you see in your own daily life (food, music, language, clothing, etc.). Trace where the cultural element originated, how it spread, and how — if at all — it changed as it moved into new regions.

modules.title: Political Geography
modules.objectives: Distinguish between different types of political boundaries; explain the concept of sovereignty and the nation-state; compare types of government around the world.
modules.readingNote: National Geographic Education — Nation vs. State vs. Country
modules.practiceNote: CIA World Factbook — Government Type Comparisons
modules.assignment: Choose two countries with different government types (e.g., a democracy and an authoritarian state). Using CIA World Factbook data, compare their systems of government and discuss how political structure affects citizens' daily lives.

modules.title: Economic Geography
modules.objectives: Distinguish between developed and developing economies using GDP and HDI; explain patterns of global trade and the role of supply chains; analyze how geography influences economic activity.
modules.practiceNote: CIA World Factbook — GDP and Economic Data
modules.assignment: Compare the GDP per capita and main industries of one high-income country and one low-income country using CIA World Factbook data. Identify one geographic advantage and one geographic challenge that each country faces in its economic development.

modules.title: North America & Europe
modules.objectives: Identify key countries, physical features, and climate zones of North America and Europe; compare their economies and regional challenges; explain major issues facing these regions.
modules.practiceNote: National Geographic Education — North America Overview
modules.assignment: Create a fact sheet comparing North America and Europe on: total area, population, dominant religion, major languages, primary economic sectors, and one major regional challenge each region faces today.

modules.title: Latin America & the Caribbean
modules.titleZh: 拉丁美洲与加勒比地区
modules.objectives: Describe the physical geography of Latin America including the Amazon, Andes, and Caribbean islands; explain how colonial history shaped the region; analyze biodiversity and development inequality.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Agriculture in Development Regions
modules.assignment: Explain how the legacy of European colonialism in Latin America contributed to modern economic inequality. Use two specific examples from different countries in the region.

modules.title: Sub-Saharan Africa
modules.objectives: Identify major physical regions of Sub-Saharan Africa; describe cultural diversity and key resources; analyze development challenges including poverty, conflict, and health crises.
modules.assignment: Select one Sub-Saharan African country. Using CIA World Factbook and National Geographic resources, describe its physical geography, main ethnic groups, major resources, and top two development challenges. Suggest one realistic step that could address one of those challenges.

modules.title: Middle East & North Africa
modules.objectives: Describe the physical geography of MENA including deserts and major rivers; explain the role of oil in shaping the region's economies and geopolitics; analyze water scarcity and religious diversity.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Religion in AP Human Geography
modules.assignment: Explain how water scarcity shapes daily life and political relations in the MENA region. Identify two specific countries whose water policies or disputes illustrate the issue, and describe the geographic factors behind each situation.

modules.title: South & Southeast Asia
modules.objectives: Explain how monsoons affect agriculture and settlement in South Asia; describe the cultural heritage and religious diversity of the region; analyze rapid economic development in Southeast Asia.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Population Density (South Asia context)
modules.assignment: Compare the role of monsoons in India with the role of river systems in Southeast Asia (e.g., Mekong River). How does each water pattern support agriculture and shape where people settle? Write a 200-word comparison.

modules.title: East Asia & the Pacific
modules.objectives: Identify key countries and geographic features of East Asia and the Pacific; analyze China, Japan, and South Korea as economic powers; explain environmental and sovereignty challenges facing Pacific Island nations.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Economic Geography and Development
modules.assignment: Pacific Island nations face rising sea levels from climate change, threatening their sovereignty and existence. Choose one Pacific Island nation, research its geographic situation using CIA World Factbook, and write a paragraph describing its vulnerability and what international response (if any) exists.

questions.question: Which of the five themes of geography asks 'How do humans change the environment and how does the environment change humans?'
questions.answer: Human-Environment Interaction

questions.question: Latitude lines run:
options: North–south, measuring distance from the prime meridian
East–west, measuring distance from the equator
questions.answer: East–west, measuring distance from the equator

questions.question: Tectonic plates move because of:
questions.answer: Convection currents in Earth's mantle

questions.question: The Himalayas were formed by:
options: A convergent boundary between the Indian and Eurasian plates
questions.answer: A convergent boundary between the Indian and Eurasian plates

questions.question: The Köppen climate classification system categorizes climates primarily based on:
questions.answer: Temperature and precipitation patterns

questions.question: Which climate zone receives less than 250 mm of precipitation per year and experiences extreme temperature swings?
questions.answer: Arid

questions.question: The tropical rainforest biome is characterized by:
options: High biodiversity, year-round warmth, and heavy rainfall
questions.answer: High biodiversity, year-round warmth, and heavy rainfall

questions.question: The tundra biome is found primarily:
questions.answer: In polar and high-altitude regions

questions.question: Population density is calculated as:
options: Total population divided by total area
Total area divided by total population
Urban population divided by rural population
questions.answer: Total population divided by total area

questions.question: Urbanization refers to:
options: The shift of people from rural areas to cities
questions.answer: The shift of people from rural areas to cities

questions.question: Cultural diffusion is best described as:
options: The isolation of one culture from outside influences
The spread of cultural elements from one group to another
The extinction of a minority language
questions.answer: The spread of cultural elements from one group to another

questions.question: Which is the most widely spoken language in the world by total speakers?
questions.answer: Mandarin Chinese

questions.question: Sovereignty means a state has:
options: Supreme authority over its own territory and affairs
questions.answer: Supreme authority over its own territory and affairs

questions.question: Explain the difference between a 'nation' and a 'state' in political geography, and give one real-world example of a nation without a state.
questions.answer: A 'state' is a politically organized territory with sovereignty; a 'nation' is a group of people sharing common culture, language, or history. An example of a nation without a state is the Kurds, a people with a distinct culture spread across Turkey, Iraq, Iran, and Syria without their own sovereign country.

questions.question: GDP per capita is most useful for comparing:
options: Average economic output per person across countries
The size of a country's military
questions.answer: Average economic output per person across countries

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an absolute location?
Which major landform is created when two tectonic plates diverge on land?
questions.answer: Rift valley

questions.question: A region near the equator with high temperatures year-round and two distinct wet/dry seasons is classified as:
questions.answer: Tropical savanna

questions.question: Which biome stores the most terrestrial carbon?
questions.answer: Tropical rainforest

questions.question: The demographic transition model predicts that as countries develop economically:
options: Birth rates and death rates both rise
Birth rates fall and death rates rise
Birth rates and death rates both decline over time
Death rates fall first, then birth rates decline
questions.answer: Death rates fall first, then birth rates decline

questions.question: Globalization has most directly accelerated cultural diffusion by:
options: Expanding internet access and mass media worldwide
questions.answer: Expanding internet access and mass media worldwide

questions.question: A unitary government system is one in which:
options: Power is shared equally between states and the federal government
Central government holds most political power
Multiple political parties share executive power
questions.answer: Central government holds most political power

questions.question: A global supply chain means that a product's components are:
options: Produced in multiple countries and assembled elsewhere
Traded only within a single trade bloc
questions.answer: Produced in multiple countries and assembled elsewhere

questions.question: The Rocky Mountains are located in:
questions.answer: North America

questions.question: The Amazon River basin, the world's largest by discharge, is located in:
questions.answer: South America

questions.question: Sub-Saharan Africa is rich in mineral resources yet faces high poverty rates. This contradiction is sometimes called:
questions.answer: The resource curse

questions.question: Water scarcity in the Middle East and North Africa is primarily caused by:
options: Arid climate, high evaporation, and rapidly growing populations
questions.answer: Arid climate, high evaporation, and rapidly growing populations

questions.question: The monsoon is a seasonal wind pattern that brings heavy rainfall primarily to:
questions.answer: South and Southeast Asia

questions.question: China's one-child policy (1980–2015) was a response to:
questions.answer: Rapid population growth straining resources

questions.question: Pacific Island nations like Tuvalu and Kiribati face an existential threat from:
options: Rising sea levels due to climate change
questions.answer: Rising sea levels due to climate change

questions.question: Japan's economy is best described as:
options: A major industrial and technological power with limited natural resources
Still classified as a developing economy
questions.answer: A major industrial and technological power with limited natural resources

questions.question: Nigeria is significant in Sub-Saharan Africa primarily because:
options: It has the highest GDP per capita in Africa
It is Africa's most populous country and a major oil producer
questions.answer: It is Africa's most populous country and a major oil producer

questions.question: The Green Revolution in South Asia (1960s–1970s) increased agricultural output through:
options: High-yield crop varieties, irrigation, and fertilizers
questions.answer: High-yield crop varieties, irrigation, and fertilizers

questions.question: Choose any two world regions studied in this course. Compare how physical geography (landforms, climate, or water resources) has shaped human settlement and economic activity in each region.
questions.answer: Answers will vary. Example: South Asia's monsoon-fed river plains (Indus, Ganges) support dense agricultural settlements and have historically driven rice and wheat economies, while MENA's arid climate concentrates settlement near rivers (Nile, Tigris-Euphrates) and has driven oil-based economies in areas lacking rivers. Students should identify specific physical features and link them to settlement patterns and economic activities.

questions.question: Which organization is primarily responsible for promoting free trade among its member nations through tariff reductions and trade agreements?
questions.answer: World Trade Organization (WTO)

quizQuestions.question: Which of the five themes of geography refers to a specific spot on Earth identified by coordinates?
Lines of latitude are also called:
GPS stands for:
The prime meridian is at:
A thematic map shows:
options: A specific topic or theme such as rainfall or population
quizQuestions.answer: A specific topic or theme such as rainfall or population

quizQuestions.question: How many continents does Earth have?
Which landform is an elevated flat-topped area?
Tectonic plates that move apart from each other form:
The Nile River flows into the:
Earthquakes are most common along:
Weather refers to:
options: Long-term atmospheric patterns in an area
Short-term atmospheric conditions at a specific time and place
quizQuestions.answer: Short-term atmospheric conditions at a specific time and place

quizQuestions.question: Tropical climate zones are located:
The main driver of global wind and ocean current patterns is:
options: Unequal heating of Earth's surface by the sun
quizQuestions.answer: Unequal heating of Earth's surface by the sun

quizQuestions.question: A rain shadow occurs when:
options: Mountains block moist air, creating dry conditions on the leeward side
Polar air masses reach the tropics
quizQuestions.answer: Mountains block moist air, creating dry conditions on the leeward side

quizQuestions.question: The polar climate zone is characterized by:
options: Year-round snow and ice with very low temperatures
quizQuestions.answer: Year-round snow and ice with very low temperatures

quizQuestions.question: A biome is best defined as:
options: A large naturally occurring community of plants and animals adapted to a specific climate
quizQuestions.answer: A large naturally occurring community of plants and animals adapted to a specific climate

quizQuestions.question: The Amazon rainforest is located primarily in:
Which biome receives the least precipitation?
Permafrost is a feature of which biome?
Deforestation of tropical rainforests is especially damaging because:
options: It destroys high-biodiversity habitats and releases stored carbon
It makes land more fertile permanently
quizQuestions.answer: It destroys high-biodiversity habitats and releases stored carbon

quizQuestions.question: The most densely populated continent is:
The natural rate of population increase equals:
options: Total fertility rate divided by population

quizQuestions.question: Push factors in migration refer to:
options: Negative conditions in the origin that drive people to leave
Cultural ties to the destination country
quizQuestions.answer: Negative conditions in the origin that drive people to leave

quizQuestions.question: A megacity is defined as a city with a population of:
Which region has the fastest population growth rate today?
Which is the most widely spoken language family in the world?
A lingua franca is:
options: A language spoken only in France
A common language used between speakers of different native languages
quizQuestions.answer: A common language used between speakers of different native languages

quizQuestions.question: The spread of McDonald's restaurants around the world is an example of:
The religion with the most followers worldwide is:
Ethnic geography studies:
options: The spatial distribution of ethnic groups and their cultural landscapes
quizQuestions.answer: The spatial distribution of ethnic groups and their cultural landscapes

quizQuestions.question: A nation-state is:
options: Any large country with a big military
A political unit in which the state's borders match the cultural nation
A country ruled by a king
quizQuestions.answer: A political unit in which the state's borders match the cultural nation

quizQuestions.question: A federal system of government divides power between:
The United Nations has how many member states (approximately as of 2024)?
A landlocked country is one that:
options: Has no direct access to a sea or ocean
quizQuestions.answer: Has no direct access to a sea or ocean

quizQuestions.question: Gerrymandering refers to:
options: Drawing electoral district boundaries to favor a political party
Moving a national capital to a new city
A type of federal voting system
quizQuestions.answer: Drawing electoral district boundaries to favor a political party

quizQuestions.question: GDP stands for:
Which sector of the economy involves extracting natural resources?
Comparative advantage means a country should:
options: Specialize in goods it can produce most efficiently relative to others
Copy the economy of the wealthiest nation
quizQuestions.answer: Specialize in goods it can produce most efficiently relative to others

quizQuestions.question: The HDI (Human Development Index) measures:
options: Life expectancy, education, and income combined
quizQuestions.answer: Life expectancy, education, and income combined

quizQuestions.question: Special Economic Zones (SEZs) attract foreign investment by offering:
The Great Plains of North America are best described as:
options: A broad, flat, fertile interior grassland
quizQuestions.answer: A broad, flat, fertile interior grassland

quizQuestions.question: The European Union (EU) is primarily:
options: A political and economic union of European nations
A free-trade zone only with no political structure
quizQuestions.answer: A political and economic union of European nations

quizQuestions.question: Which body of water separates Europe from North Africa?
The Mississippi River drains into the:
Canada and the United States share the world's:
The Andes Mountains run along the western coast of:
The Amazon River carries approximately what fraction of Earth's river water to the sea?
Most Latin American countries gained independence from which two colonial powers?
The Caribbean Sea is part of which ocean?
High inequality in Latin America is partly measured by the Gini coefficient, which measures:
The Sahara Desert forms a natural boundary between North Africa and:
The Congo River Basin contains the world's second largest:
Africa has the world's largest number of:
The Great Rift Valley is associated with:
options: A series of lakes and volcanic mountains running through East Africa
A flat plateau in Southern Africa
A river delta in West Africa
quizQuestions.answer: A series of lakes and volcanic mountains running through East Africa

quizQuestions.question: Which country is Africa's largest by land area?
The Sahara Desert is located in:
OPEC (Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries) was formed primarily to:
options: Distribute oil equally to all nations
Coordinate oil production policies among major oil-exporting nations
Create a military alliance in the Gulf
Regulate oil prices exclusively for Western nations
quizQuestions.answer: Coordinate oil production policies among major oil-exporting nations

quizQuestions.question: The two most important rivers in the Middle East for agriculture and human settlement are:
Israel, Jordan, and Palestine all claim religious and historical significance to which city?
Desalination is important in MENA because the region:
options: Has too much groundwater to use
Lacks sufficient freshwater but borders seas that can be processed
Has the world's largest river systems
Relies entirely on imported bottled water
quizQuestions.answer: Lacks sufficient freshwater but borders seas that can be processed

quizQuestions.question: A monsoon is a:
options: Seasonal reversal of wind direction causing wet and dry seasons
Mountain wind found only in the Himalayas
quizQuestions.answer: Seasonal reversal of wind direction causing wet and dry seasons

quizQuestions.question: India and China together account for approximately what fraction of the world's population?
The Mekong River flows through several Southeast Asian countries before emptying into:
Which Southeast Asian country has the largest population?
The Hindu-Buddhist cultural tradition spread across South and Southeast Asia primarily through:
Which country has the world's largest population as of the mid-2020s?
Japan is an archipelago, meaning it is:
options: A country on a large peninsula

quizQuestions.question: The 'Ring of Fire' that runs through East Asia and the Pacific is associated with:
South Korea's rapid economic development after the 1950s Korean War is often called:
Which ocean surrounds most Pacific Island nations?
